NAME

SYNOPSIS

DESCRIPTION

This interface is obsoleted by setpriority(2) . The function obtains the scheduling priority of the process from the system and sets it to the priority value specified in The priority is a value in the range -20 to 20. The default priority is 0; lower priorities cause more favorable scheduling. Only the super-user may lower priorities. Children inherit the priority of their parent processes via

SEE ALSO

HISTORY

A syscall appeared in
